summary: Route-resolve mention edges at check against the discovered corpus; a deferred mention rides the lock verified nowhere today. Fire graph.route on a dangle; explain narrates it.

edit.description: Route-resolve mention `ResolvedEdge`s against the by-kind corpus (discovered members included) and fire a `graph.route` finding on a dangling mention target (member `kind:name` or bare requirement name). Today `resolved_mention_edges` (L882) folds every mention in as already-resolved and only `degree` consumes them, so no route check runs. Correct the now-false doc (L876-880: 'a dangling mention never reaches the lock, emit refuses first' — a deferred mention DOES ride the lock).

Wire mention route-resolution into both the `check` command path (~L489, mention_edges assembled there) and the shared `gate` (~L675/L816), so a deferred mention's verdict is owned at check against the discovered corpus — the same table `implemented-by`/route resolution reads. Correct the stale narration at L672-674 ('resolved once at emit and lifted off the lock', true pre-deferral).

explain narrates a dangling mention instead of folding every mention edge into the resolved set (L303 extends `resolved` with all mention edges unconditionally) — a mention whose target is absent from the discovered corpus must read as dangling, not resolved, matching the gate verdict (READ-EDGE-UNIFY).

tests.asserts: a mention edge whose target is absent from the by-kind corpus fires a graph.route error naming the citing member and the dangling target; a mention resolving to a discovered member stays clean
explain narrates a deferred mention to a target absent from disk as dangling, not resolved

acceptance: A deferred mention whose target is absent from the discovered corpus fires a graph.route error at check (surfaced by every reporter); explain narrates the dangle; a mention resolving to a discovered member stays clean — check owns the verdict the deferral hands it (pipeline.md Emit: the row rides the lock and check owns the verdict).
notes: Field-verified (base-harness verify-summary mentions source:main): emit defers, lock rides, check+explain stay green with src/main.js gone. resolved_mention_edges folds every mention in as resolved; graph::check ranges over declared Edges only; read.rs:303 extends resolved with all mentions. drift.rs:2472 MentionRow doc already states the target behavior; graph.rs:876 doc ('a dangling mention never reaches the lock') now false. scoped at 7a3632f.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. scoped at b676656.
